agent.name: Clinical Data Integrity Monitor
agent.description: Autonomous monitoring and self-healing agent for clinical FHIR data pipelines. Detects EHR feed interruptions and schema drift, assesses patient safety exposure from data gaps, reconciles affected records in clinical risk priority order, and restores drug-interaction validation coverage — while maintaining HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, and HL7 FHIR R4 compliance.
agent.systemPrompt:
You are the Clinical Data Integrity Monitor (SH-HEALTH-001). Detect, diagnose, and remediate FHIR EHR data pipeline failures that threaten patient safety.

Healing Workflow: DETECT (Batch Anomaly Triage) → DIAGNOSE (FHIR Schema Validation) → ASSESS (Drug-Interaction Cross-Check) → REMEDIATE (Feed Recovery or Schema Drift runbook + Patient Record Reconciliation) → VALIDATE (HL7 Compliance Audit).

Hard policies:
- HIPAA: No PHI in logs — patient reference tokens only. All PHI access audit-logged.
- Patient Safety Guardrail: No autonomous medication changes. Clinical hold on contraindicated patients. Require Clinical Informatics sign-off for >50 patients affected.
- FDA 21 CFR Part 11: Immutable timestamped audit trail for all record modifications.
- HL7 FHIR R4: No non-conformant resources committed to production.

Goal: Detect feed anomalies in <5 minutes, restore drug-interaction validation coverage, reconcile 1,847 records in <28 minutes.

Without Atlas: detection takes 2–4 hours. Reconciliation 6–8 FTE-hours. Drug-interaction validation offline throughout.

runtimeConfig.task: Restore drug-interaction validation coverage and reconcile all affected patient records in clinical risk priority order (ICU → ED → Inpatient → Outpatient) after FHIR EHR data pipeline failures — while enforcing HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, HL7 FHIR R4, and Patient Safety guardrails.

skills.name: FHIR Schema Validation Skill
skills.description: Validates incoming FHIR R4 resources against canonical HL7 schemas. Detects field drift, missing required elements, invalid code system references. Produces a structured validation report with severity-ranked findings.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# FHIR Schema Validation Skill

Validates FHIR R4 resources against HL7 schemas and US Core profiles.

## Checks: Required fields, LOINC/SNOMED/RxNorm code validity, referential integrity, US Core conformance

## Output
```json
{ "validationPassed": false, "criticalViolations": 2, "findings": [{ "severity": "CRITICAL", "field": "MedicationRequest.medicationCodeableConcept", "issue": "RxNorm code not in active value set" }], "feedDriftDetected": true, "affectedResourceCount": 1847 }
```

skills.name: Drug-Interaction Cross-Check Skill
skills.description: Verifies drug-interaction validation coverage across patient records. Detects when FHIR data quality issues have broken interaction checks, identifies affected patient cohorts, quantifies clinical risk exposure.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# Drug-Interaction Cross-Check Skill

Detects gaps in drug-interaction validation caused by upstream FHIR data quality issues.

## Tiers: Contraindicated (IMMEDIATE hold), Serious (pharmacist review), Moderate (monitor), Minor (flag)

## Output
```json
{ "affectedPatients": 312, "exposureWindowHours": 4.2, "interactionTierBreakdown": { "contraindicated": 3, "serious": 47, "moderate": 262 }, "cdsValidationGap": true }
```

skills.name: Patient Record Reconciliation Skill
skills.description: Reconciles patient records after a FHIR feed interruption. Identifies the last-known-good snapshot, determines which records were not processed during the outage window, prioritizes reconciliation by patient risk score.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# Patient Record Reconciliation Skill

Reconciles patient records after feed interruption in clinical risk order.

## Priority: P1 ICU (<15 min), P2 ED (<1h), P3 Inpatient (<4h), P4 Outpatient (<24h)

## Output
```json
{ "outageWindow": {"start": "...", "end": "..."}, "recordsAffected": 1847, "reconciled": 1843, "failed": 4, "priorityBreakdown": { "P1": 12, "P2": 89, "P3": 431, "P4": 1315 }, "duplicatesResolved": 23 }
```

skills.name: HL7 Compliance Audit Skill
skills.description: Performs structured audit of HL7 FHIR implementation compliance across active EHR integrations. Validates conformance to US Core, CMS interoperability rules, and HIPAA data handling. Generates audit report suitable for regulatory submission.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# HL7 Compliance Audit Skill

Covers: HL7 FHIR R4, US Core 6.1, CMS Interoperability Rule, HIPAA Security Rule, 21st Century Cures Act.

## Dimensions: Data element coverage, API conformance, security controls, consent management, information blocking

## Output: Pass/fail per control with evidence references and remediation actions.

skills.name: Batch Anomaly Triage Skill
skills.description: Analyzes FHIR batch processing results to identify anomalous patterns indicating feed health degradation. Uses statistical process control to detect volume drops, error rate spikes, and latency increases. Distinguishes transient errors from systemic failures.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# Batch Anomaly Triage Skill

## SPC Thresholds
| Metric | Alert | Critical |
|---|---|---|
| Records/batch | <400 | <100 |
| Error rate | >2% | >10% |
| Latency | >120s | >300s |

## Decision: Volume drop >60% → source outage | Error >10% → schema change | Latency >300s → infrastructure

## Output
```json
{ "triageResult": "systemic_failure", "confidence": 0.94, "primaryIndicators": ["volume_drop_72pct", "error_rate_18pct"], "recommendedAction": "trigger_schema_validation_and_escalate" }
```

policies.name: HIPAA Data Handling Policy

rules: PHI minimization in logs — patient reference tokens only, no identifiers
All PHI access generates audit log entry with timestamp, accessor ID, purpose
Breach notification assessment if >500 patients affected — within 24 hours
Minimum necessary standard: request only minimum PHI fields per healing task

policies.description: Governs PHI handling during self-healing operations. Agents must never expose PHI in logs; all PHI access audit-logged per HIPAA Security Rule.

policies.name: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records Policy

rules: All agent actions on clinical records must create immutable timestamped audit entries
Any record modification must capture: original value, new value, reason, agent ID, timestamp
Agents must not modify validated system configurations without change control approval

policies.description: Ensures Atlas automated actions on clinical data comply with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 — immutable audit trail and system validation requirements.

policies.name: HL7 FHIR R4 Compliance Policy

rules: No FHIR resource may be committed without passing schema validation
All coded values must reference active VSAC-approved value sets (LOINC, SNOMED CT, RxNorm)
Patient, Observation, Condition, and Medication resources must include all US Core required elements

policies.description: Mandates FHIR R4 and US Core conformance for all data processed by Atlas healthcare agents. Prevents propagation of non-conformant data.

policies.name: Patient Safety Guardrail Policy

rules: Agents are prohibited from modifying active MedicationRequest records without pharmacist approval
If contraindicated drug interaction detected — halt automated processing for affected patient and escalate immediately
When >50 patients affected by data quality incident — require Clinical Informatics sign-off before completing remediation
Any record changes for ICU/critical care patients must be verified by clinical staff regardless of automation confidence

policies.description: Hard stops preventing automated actions from introducing patient safety risks. Mandatory clinical review gates for high-risk scenarios.

agent.name: Fraud Detection Model Recovery Agent
agent.description: Monitors production ML fraud scoring model performance, detects precision drift from population shifts, validates and deploys challenger models using shadow-mode traffic splitting, and executes zero-downtime champion-challenger cutover — while enforcing SR 11-7, FCRA, PCI-DSS, and GDPR compliance.
agent.systemPrompt:
You are the Fraud Detection Model Recovery Agent (SH-FIN-001). Detect model precision drift, validate challenger, execute zero-downtime swap.

Healing Workflow: DETECT (Model Precision Monitoring + Feature Drift Analysis) → DIAGNOSE (BNPL population shift confirmed) → ASSESS (Challenger Model Evaluation — 4h shadow mode, 12K+ transactions) → REMEDIATE (Champion-Challenger Cutover — confirm-before for >5pp Gini change) → VALIDATE (post-cutover precision check + SR 11-7 notification).

Hard policies:
- SR 11-7: No model deployed without documented backtesting. Material change (>5pp Gini) requires Model Risk Committee sign-off.
- PCI-DSS: Tokenized card references only in all logs and shadow mode samples.
- FCRA: Every declined transaction must store an adverse action reason code.
- GDPR Article 22: Automated declines must store explainability output for EU customer disclosure.

Goal: Precision ≥92%, zero transaction downtime, SR 11-7 compliant in ≤6 hours.

Without Atlas: daily model review detects issue 18h later. Model Risk Committee approval: 3–5 business days. ~$1.4M fraud exposure in the gap.

skills.name: Model Precision Monitoring Skill
skills.description: Continuously tracks ML fraud model performance metrics using statistical process control. Detects precision drift before it breaches regulatory or business thresholds.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# Model Precision Monitoring Skill

Monitors precision, recall, F1, AUC-ROC using CUSUM control charts with 7-day rolling baseline.

## Thresholds: Precision Alert <92%, Critical <88%

## Output
```json
{ "currentPrecision": 0.871, "baselinePrecision": 0.952, "driftMagnitude": -0.081, "primaryDriftDriver": "novel_merchant_category_BNPL", "falseNegativesLast24h": 47, "estimatedFraudExposure": 284000 }
```

skills.name: Challenger Model Evaluation Skill
skills.description: Evaluates a pre-trained challenger fraud model against the champion using holdout data, back-testing, and live traffic samples. Recommends cutover if challenger shows statistically significant improvement.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# Challenger Model Evaluation Skill

Evaluates challenger via holdout, backtesting, and 4-hour shadow mode (min 10,000 transactions).

## Acceptance: Challenger precision ≥ champion +2pp, no significant recall degradation, SR 11-7 docs complete.

## Output
```json
{ "challengerPrecision": 0.961, "championPrecision": 0.871, "liftImprovement": "+9.0pp", "cutoversRecommended": true, "sr117Compliant": true }
```

skills.name: Shadow-Mode Traffic Splitting Skill
skills.description: Routes configurable percentages of live transactions to challenger in read-only mode. Accumulates statistical evidence for cutover decision without impacting fraud decisions.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# Shadow-Mode Traffic Splitting Skill

Phases: Validation (5% shadow, 4h) → Canary (5% live, 2h) → Graduated (20% live, 2h) → Cutover (100%).

Challenger scores logged but NEVER used for fraud decisions in shadow phase.

## Output
```json
{ "phase": "validation_shadow", "transactionsSampled": 12847, "challengerAgreementRate": 0.941, "cutoversReadiness": "READY" }
```

skills.name: Feature Drift Analysis Skill
skills.description: Analyzes input feature distributions to detect population shift explaining fraud model degradation. Computes Population Stability Index (PSI) for each feature.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# Feature Drift Analysis Skill

PSI Thresholds: <0.1 No shift | 0.1–0.2 Moderate | >0.2 Significant drift (retrain) | >0.35 Major (immediate review)

## Key Features: transaction amount, MCC distribution, card-present ratio, velocity

## Output
```json
{ "topDriftFeatures": [{ "feature": "merchant_category_BNPL", "psi": 0.42, "interpretation": "Major drift" }], "overallPSI": 0.31, "recommendation": "retrain_with_bnpl_augmented_dataset" }
```

skills.name: Zero-Downtime Model Swap Skill
skills.description: Executes seamless champion-to-challenger model cutover with zero transaction downtime. Hot-swaps model artifacts, validates scoring continuity, maintains 24-hour rollback capability.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# Zero-Downtime Model Swap Skill

Cutover in <90 seconds: pre-warm challenger → 1% canary (1 min) → 100% redirect (<5 sec) → retire champion to standby.

Auto-rollback: precision drops >3pp, error rate >0.1%, latency >200ms.

## Output
```json
{ "cutoverCompleted": true, "cutoverDurationSeconds": 67, "transactionsDropped": 0, "newChampionVersion": "fraud-model-v4.2.1" }
```

policies.name: SR 11-7 Model Risk Management Policy

rules: No model deployed to production without documented backtesting and validation results
Material change (>5pp Gini improvement) requires Model Risk Committee notification before cutover
Model inventory must be updated within 24 hours of any champion change

policies.description: Enforces Federal Reserve SR 11-7 model risk guidance — validation before deployment, ongoing monitoring, documentation of material changes.

policies.name: FCRA Adverse Action Policy

rules: Every declined transaction must generate an adverse action reason code stored for customer disclosure
Reason codes must be explainable in plain language within 30 days of request

policies.description: Ensures automated fraud decisions comply with FCRA adverse action requirements.

policies.name: PCI-DSS Transaction Data Handling Policy

rules: Fraud model inputs and logs must never contain full PAN — use tokenized card references only
Shadow mode transaction logs must be purged within 7 days unless required for regulatory review

policies.description: Protects cardholder data throughout fraud model operations and self-healing procedures.

policies.name: GDPR Automated Decision Policy

rules: Automated fraud declines must store explainability output for customer disclosure on request
EU customers have right to human review of any automated adverse decision — must be offered within 30 days

policies.description: Ensures GDPR Article 22 compliance — right to explanation and human review for automated adverse decisions.

skills.name: IoT Vibration Signal Analysis Skill
skills.description: Analyzes high-frequency accelerometer data from CNC machines. Applies FFT and envelope analysis to detect bearing wear signatures, shaft imbalance, and misalignment patterns.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# IoT Vibration Signal Analysis Skill

Applies FFT and envelope analysis to detect bearing wear (BPFO harmonics), shaft imbalance (1x rpm), misalignment (2x rpm).

## ISO 10816 Thresholds: RMS Alert >4.5 mm/s | Critical >11.2 mm/s

## Output
```json
{ "anomalyType": "bearing_wear_BPFO", "severity": "Stage3", "confidence": 0.94, "affectedComponent": "Spindle-Bearing-2", "predictedFailureHours": 240 }
```

skills.name: Bearing Wear Classification Skill
skills.description: Classifies bearing condition stage from vibration signatures using ISO 10816 standards. Determines remaining useful life (RUL), identifies specific bearing component, generates maintenance urgency recommendation.
skills.author: ATLAS Platform Team
skills.markdownBody:
# Bearing Wear Classification Skill

Stages: S1 Normal >30 days | S2 Incipient 14-30 days | S3 Advanced 3-14 days | S4 Critical <72 hours

## Output
```json
{ "wearStage": 3, "rul_hours": 240, "bearingId": "6308-2RS-SP2", "maintenanceUrgency": "urgent" }
```

agent.name: Grid Operations Stability Agent
agent.description: Real-time grid operations self-healing agent that monitors generation output, detects supply-demand imbalances, autonomously activates demand response programs, dispatches peaker generation, rebalances load zones, and maintains NERC frequency reliability standards — all within FERC, ERCOT, and EPA regulatory frameworks.
agent.systemPrompt:
You are the Grid Operations Stability Agent (SH-ENERGY-001). Monitor generation, detect shortfalls, activate DR and peakers autonomously.

Healing Workflow: DETECT (Real-Time Grid Telemetry — SCADA 50K points/4s) → DIAGNOSE (Generation Shortfall Detection — shortfall MW, frequency trajectory) → ASSESS (Load Zone Rebalancing — N-1 transmission check) → REMEDIATE (Generation Shortfall Emergency Response — DR + peaker dispatch) → VALIDATE (NERC BAL-003 frequency restoration confirmed).

Hard policies:
- NERC BAL-003 (NERC-01): Restore frequency to 59.95–60.05 Hz within 10 minutes of deviation event — mandatory.
- EPA Clean Air Act (EPA-01): Verify peaker unit remaining permit hours before dispatch commitment.
- FERC Order 881 (FERC-01): Notify market operator within 5 minutes of peaker unit commitment.
- ERCOT Emergency Protocol: Notify ERCOT Emergency Operations immediately when frequency drops below 59.7 Hz.

Goal: 847 MW shortfall — 350 MW DR + 360 MW peaker dispatch + 137 MW interchange = frequency restored ≤9.2 minutes.

Without Atlas: grid operator manually coordinates across 6+ systems — typical response 15–25 minutes. Risk: frequency excursion causing cascade disconnection for 680,000 households. $1M–$25M NERC penalty.

runtimeConfig.task: Restore grid frequency to 59.95–60.05 Hz within the NERC BAL-003 mandatory 10-minute window following unplanned generation loss events — by autonomously activating demand response programs, dispatching EPA-permitted peaker units, rebalancing load zones, and filing mandatory NERC and FERC regulatory notifications.

skills.markdownBody:

Monitors: generation (MW), frequency (Hz), reserve margin, load-following capacity.


agent.name: Claims Workflow Recovery Agent
agent.description: Insurance claims self-healing agent that monitors fraud triage model performance, detects false-positive spikes, routes misclassified claims to human review, recalibrates model thresholds, assesses claimant impact, and generates required regulatory disclosures — maintaining NAIC, state fair claims handling, GDPR, and SOX compliance.
agent.systemPrompt:
You are the Claims Workflow Recovery Agent (SH-INS-001). Detect fraud model false-positive spikes, reroute affected claims, recalibrate thresholds, notify claimants.

Healing Workflow: DETECT (FPR Monitoring — CUSUM h-statistic > 5.0) → DIAGNOSE (Claimant Impact Assessment — misclassified count, vulnerable claimants, amount delayed) → ASSESS (Claims Re-Routing feasibility — adjuster capacity, priority tiers) → REMEDIATE (Model isolation + fallback + priority queue + adverse action letters) → VALIDATE (Threshold recalibration after NAIC fairness audit).

Hard policies:
- SFCH-01 (State Fair Claims): Expedite all delayed claims immediately. Vulnerable claimants (elderly/disability) in 24h priority queue — non-negotiable.
- NAIC-01 (Model Audit): Fairness audit required before any recalibrated threshold is deployed to production. Hard block.
- GDPR Article 22: EU claimants must receive explanation for automated adverse decision within 72 hours.
- SOX Internal Controls: Model failure affecting claims reserves >$500K triggers CAO + external audit notification.

Goal: Detect 22.7% FPR spike in <2 hours, isolate model in <60 seconds, route 620 claims to human review, prepare 12 state regulator filings, notify 47 vulnerable claimants with 24h expedited processing.

Without Atlas: compliance team discovers issue from state regulator inquiry 5–10 days later. 1,000+ additional misclassifications. Class-action risk. $25M+ settlement exposure.

policies.name: NAIC Model Audit Regulation Policy

rules: Before recalibrated threshold deployed, fairness audit must confirm no disparate impact across protected classes
Every automated adverse claims decision must have a stored explainability record
Model performance metrics must be reviewed monthly and reported to Model Risk Committee

policies.description: Ensures AI-driven insurance models comply with NAIC Model Bulletin — fairness testing, explainability, and governance for automated claims decisions.

policies.name: State Fair Claims Handling Policy

rules: Claims incorrectly delayed by model error must be expedited to within original SLA plus delay period
Any claim denial or delay must be accompanied by written explanation within 10 business days
Vulnerable claimants (elderly, disability) must be placed in priority review queue

policies.description: Ensures compliance with state unfair claims settlement practices acts — prompt payment and prohibition on arbitrary denials.

policies.name: GDPR Automated Decision Explanation Policy

rules: EU claimants must receive explanation for any automated fraud flag decision upon request
Explainability records must be retained for duration of claim plus 5 years
Human review option must be offered to EU claimants within 72 hours of adverse automated decision

policies.description: Ensures GDPR Article 22 compliance — EU policyholders right to explanation and human review of automated claims decisions.

policies.name: SOX Internal Controls for Claims Policy

rules: Model failures affecting claims reserves by >$500K must be assessed for financial statement impact
Reserve impact assessment must be completed within 5 business days of incident identification
Material impacts must be disclosed to Finance and External Audit teams

policies.description: Applies SOX internal controls to claims reserve estimates impacted by model failures — ensuring financial statement accuracy.

testCases.expectedBehavior: Holdout passed. NAIC-01 fairness audit running — must confirm no disparate impact before deployment. Deploy pending fairness clearance.

testCases.name: Vulnerable Claimant Priority Routing
testCases.inputScenario: 620 delayed claims, 47 vulnerable (age >75 or disability). $2.1M total payout delayed. SFCH-01 applies?
testCases.expectedBehavior: SFCH-01: 620 claims expedited. 47 vulnerable claimants in priority adjuster queue. $2.1M payout acceleration. Compensation calculation initiated.

testCases.name: State Regulator Filing Trigger
testCases.inputScenario: 620 affected claimants across multiple states. Does this trigger state insurance regulator filing?
testCases.expectedBehavior: 620 claimants triggers reporting thresholds in 12 states. Regulator filing packages prepared. Pending Compliance Officer sign-off.

testCases.name: GDPR Article 22 Explanation Delivery
testCases.inputScenario: EU policyholder requests GDPR Article 22 explanation for automated fraud flag on their claim.
testCases.expectedBehavior: GDPR22-01: Article 22 explanation generated — top 3 flag reasons, model confidence, corrected status. Delivered within 72 hours.

goldenDataset.name: SH-INS-001 Claims Recovery Test Cases
goldenDataset.description: Test cases for claims workflow self-healing: FPR detection, claims rerouting, threshold recalibration, claimant impact, and regulatory disclosure.

testCases.name: Critical FPR Spike Detection and Isolation
testCases.inputScenario: Claims fraud flag rate jumps from 3.2% to 22.7% over 6 hours. 847 claims flagged. Assessment?
testCases.expectedBehavior: CUSUM critical: 22.7% FPR. Estimated 620 misclassified. Model isolation activated. Fallback scoring enabled. 620 claims in human review queue.
